Final Project

Melanie Kos

Read in data, cleaning genie

Read in the dataframes and cleaning genies

subject_df <- read_csv("/Users/tur61139/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TempleUniversity/IMENSA/unpacked_from_D/subject_df.csv")
## Rows: 88 Columns: 602
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (11): SubID, Visit1_date, Visit2_date, Gender, Race, HealthHistory, Far...
## dbl (591): Condition, Sex, Age, Hispanic, Handedness, Height, Weight, AAS_1,...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
fast_long_df <- read_csv("/Users/tur61139/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TempleUniversity/IMENSA/unpacked_from_D/subject_fast_long_df.csv")
## Rows: 2640 Columns: 26
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (25): Fast1_teardrop, Fast1_pain, Fast1_happiness, Fast1_mirror, Fast1_k...
## dbl  (1): subject_index
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
original_interview_df <- read_excel("/Users/tur61139/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TempleUniversity/IMENSA/ORIGINAL + CLEANED IMENSA Exit Interview Data.xlsx", sheet = "Original")
cleaned_interview_df <- read_excel("/Users/tur61139/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TempleUniversity/IMENSA/ORIGINAL + CLEANED IMENSA Exit Interview Data.xlsx", sheet = "Cleaned")

melanie_genie <- function(data, # Input data
                          text_col = NULL, # Indicate name of text column for a df input
                          id_col = NULL, # Indicate name of subject ID column for a df input
                          lemmatize = TRUE, #Lemmatize words on or off
                          omit_stops = TRUE, # Remove stops
                          remove_numbers = TRUE, # Remove numbers 
                          remove_number_words = TRUE, # Remove written out numbers
                          remove_roman = TRUE, # Remove roman numerals
                          strip_possessives = TRUE, # Amend singular and plural possessives
                          normalize_abbrev = TRUE, # Replace common abbreviations with full word
                          dash_mode = c("prefix_merge", "split", "remove"), 
                          # Prefix_merge = merge common list of common prefixes on hyphen (ie re-do becomes redo, well-being becomes well being) 
                          # Split = all hyphens become spaces (ie re-do becomes re do, well-being becomes well being) 
                          # Remove = all hyphens are removed (ie re-do becomes redo, well-being becomes wellbeing) 
                          return_base_text = FALSE) # Return the fully cleaned base text along with two df outputs

{
  
  dash_mode <- match.arg(dash_mode)
  
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(stringi)
  library(textstem)
  library(tidytext)
  
  ###############################################################
  ### Data type check; if data is a df, indicate the text column
  ###############################################################
  if (is.data.frame(data)) {
    
    if (is.null(text_col)) {
      stop("If data is a dataframe, you must supply text column name")}
    
    if (!text_col %in% names(data)) {
      stop(paste("Column", text_col, "not found in df"))}
    
    x <- as.character(data[[text_col]])
    if (is.null(id_col)) {
      id_values <- seq_len(nrow(data))
    } else {
      if (!id_col %in% names(data))
        stop(paste("Column", id_col, "not found in dataframe"))
      
      id_values <- data[[id_col]]
    }
    
    df_base <- tibble(ID = id_values, id_row_orig = seq_along(x), text = x)
    
  } else {
    
    # Character vector input
    x <- as.character(data)
    df_base <- tibble(ID = seq_along(x), id_row_orig = seq_along(x), text = x)
  }
  
  # Load stopword and replacement lists from github  
  load(url("https://github.com/Reilly-ConceptsCognitionLab/reillylab_publicdata/blob/main/Temple_stops25.rda?raw=true"))
  load(url("https://github.com/Reilly-ConceptsCognitionLab/reillylab_publicdata/blob/main/replacements_25.rda?raw=true"))
  ################################
  ### Below modules are always on: 
  ################################
  
  # Normalize all text to UTF-8 encoding
  utf8_normalize <- function(x) {
    tryCatch(stringi::stri_enc_toutf8(as.character(x), is_unknown_8bit = TRUE, validate = TRUE),
             error = function(e)
               stringi::stri_encode(as.character(x), to = "UTF-8"))
  }
  
  # Remove any new lines, tabs, returns, etc. 
  strip_control_characters <- function(x) {
    stringi::stri_replace_all_regex(x, "[\\p{C}]+", " ")
  }
  
  # Normalize apostrophes and quotation marks
  normalize_quotes <- function(x) {
    x <- str_replace_all(x, "[\u2018\u2019\u2032\u0027\u00b4\u201b]","'") # Manage apostrophes to be straight
    x <- str_replace_all(x,"[\u201c\u201d\u0022\u2033\u02dd\u201f\u201e]","[\u0022]") # Manage quotation marks to be straight
    x
  }
  
  #####################################
  ### Below modules are toggled on/off:
  #####################################
  
  # Normalize abbreviations (toggle on/off)
  normalize_abbrev_module <- function(x) {
    # Apply contractions replacement (only for non-NA values) (search x for contractions, replace with replacement phrase)
    x <- str_replace_all(x, setNames(replacements_25$replacement, replacements_25$word))
    
    # Manual change of popular abbreviations... 
    x <- gsub("and/or", "and or", x)
    x <- gsub("\\bi\\.e\\.(?=\\W|$)", "that is", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\betc\\.(?=\\W|$)", "etcetera", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("vis-a-vis(?=\\W|$)", "visavis", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\be\\.g\\.(?=\\W|$)", "for example", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bMr\\.(?=\\W|$)", "mister", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bMrs\\.(?=\\W|$)", "missus", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bMs\\.(?=\\W|$)", "miss", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bSr\\.(?=\\W|$)", "senior", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bJr\\.(?=\\W|$)", "junior", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bSte\\.(?=\\W|$)", "suite", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bCapt\\.(?=\\W|$)", "captain", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bProf\\.(?=\\W|$)", "professor", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bRev\\.(?=\\W|$)", "reverend", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("\\bSt\\.(?=\\s+[A-Z])", "saint", x, perl = TRUE) # Saint (religious / place names), usually followed by capital letter for name
    x <- gsub("\\bSt\\.(?=\\s*[a-z0-9]|\\W|$)", "street", x, perl = TRUE) # Street (addresses), usually followed by non-capital letter or punctuation
    x <- gsub("\\bDr\\.(?=\\s+[A-Z])", "doctor", x, perl = TRUE) # Doctor, usually followed by capital letter for name
    x <- gsub("\\bDr\\.(?=\\s*[a-z0-9]|\\W|$)", "drive", x, perl = TRUE) # Drive (addresses), usually followed by non-capital letter or punctuation
    x
  }
  
  # Normalize singular and plural possessives (toggle on/off)
  strip_possessives_module <- function(x) {
    x <- gsub("'s\\b", "", x) # Singular possessive (so no additional s at end of word)
    x <- gsub("s'\\b", "s", x) # Plural possessives (so no additional ss at end of word)
    x
  }
  
  # Remove roman numerals for chapters, etc. AND other numerals anywhere else (toggle on/off)
  remove_roman_module <- function(x) {
    # Remove chapter, section numerals
    x <- gsub("\\b(?i:(chapter|section|act|book|part))\\s+(?i:[ivxlcdm]+)(st|nd|rd|th)?(?=[[:punct:]]|\\s|$)", "", x, perl = TRUE)
    
    # Remove any remaining Roman numerals anywhere in text
    x <- gsub("\\b(?i:[ivxlcdm]+)(st|nd|rd|th)?\\b", "", x,perl = TRUE)
    x
  }
  
  # Remove all numeric numbers and suffix for written numbers (toggle on/off)
  remove_numbers_module <- function(x) {
    x <- gsub("\\b(\\d+)(st|nd|rd|th)\\b", "\\1", x, perl = TRUE)
    x <- gsub("[[:digit:]]", "", x)
    x
  }
  
  
  # Remove all written out numbers (toggle on/off)
  remove_number_words_module <- function(words) {
    number_stems <- "\\b(zero|one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|eleven|twelve|thirteen|fourteen|fifteen|sixteen|seventeen|eighteen|nineteen|twenty|thirty|forty|fifty|sixty|seventy|eighty|ninety|hundred|thousand|million|billion)\\b"
    ifelse(str_detect(words, number_stems), NA_character_, words)
  }
  
  
  # Hyphen/dash module, 3 potential modes (also defined above): 
  # Prefix_merge = merge common list of common prefixes on hyphen (ie re-do becomes redo, well-being becomes well being) 
  # Split = all hyphens become spaces (ie re-do becomes re do, well-being becomes well being) 
  # Remove = all hyphens are removed and squished (ie re-do becomes redo, well-being becomes wellbeing) 
  dash_mode_module <- function(x, mode) {
    if (mode == "prefix_merge") {
      x <- gsub(
        "\\b(re|co|de|ex|pro|anti|non|self|pre|post|un|trans|quasi|dis|im|auto|hyper|neo|under|over|sub|mis|poly|mal|in|inter|com|bi|counter|be|good|to|with|a|al|be|for|fore|out)\\s*-\\s*",
        "\\1", x, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
      x <- gsub("(?<=\\p{L})-(?=\\p{L})", " ", x, perl = TRUE) # Any other words without above prefixes become two words
    }
    if (mode == "split") {
      x <- gsub("-", " ", x) # Split all hyphens into two words 
    }
    if (mode == "remove") {
      x <- gsub("-", "", x) # Split all hyphens into one word
    }
    x
  }
  
  # Lemmatization module (toggle on/off)
  lemmatize_module <- function(text_vector) {
    textstem::lemmatize_strings(text_vector) # Note lemmatize_strings usage
  }
  
  # Remove stop words as defined in Temple_stops25 (toggle on/off, can also change stop word list...)
  omit_stops_module <- function(words) {
    stopwords <- tolower(Temple_stops25$word)
    ifelse(words %in% stopwords, NA_character_, words)
  }
  
  #############################################
  # Actual cleaning begins...
  #############################################
  df_base$text <- utf8_normalize(df_base$text)
  df_base$text <- strip_control_characters(df_base$text)
  df_base$text <- normalize_quotes(df_base$text)
  
  if (normalize_abbrev) {
    df_base$text <- normalize_abbrev_module(df_base$text)}
  
  if (strip_possessives) {
    df_base$text <- strip_possessives_module(df_base$text)}
  
  # Roman numeral removal BEFORE punctuation cleanup (so "I." "II." etc. are removed)
  if (remove_roman) {
    df_base$text <- remove_roman_module(df_base$text)}
  
  # Dash/hyphen handling
  df_base$text <- dash_mode_module(df_base$text, dash_mode)
  
  # Punctuation cleanup
  df_base$text <- gsub("\t|[[:punct:]]+", " ", df_base$text) # Remove tabs, punctuation, account for any missing spaces between punctuation
  
  # Lowercase and squish
  df_base$text <- str_to_lower(df_base$text) # Lowercase everything
  df_base$text <- str_squish(df_base$text) # Remove any empty spaces between words, line breaks, etc. 
  
  # Digit removal and numeric suffix removal AFTER punctuation cleanup 
  if (remove_numbers) {
    df_base$text <- remove_numbers_module(df_base$text)}
  
  # String-level lemmatization BEFORE tokenization
  if (lemmatize) {
    df_base$text <- lemmatize_module(df_base$text)}
  
  # Tokenization
  token_df <- df_base |>
    unnest_tokens(word_raw, text, drop = FALSE) |>
    mutate(id_row_postsplit = row_number(),
           word_lemma = word_raw)
  
  # Remove number words
  if (remove_number_words) {
    token_df <- token_df |>
      mutate(word_lemma = remove_number_words_module(word_lemma))}
  
  
  # Stopword toggle
  if (omit_stops) {
    token_df <- token_df |>
      mutate(word_clean = omit_stops_module(word_lemma))
  } else {
    token_df <- token_df |>
      mutate(word_clean = word_lemma)}
  
  # Remove single letters
  token_df <- token_df |>
    mutate(word_clean = ifelse(nchar(word_clean) <= 1, NA_character_, word_clean))
  
  # Counts
  token_df <- token_df |>
    mutate(word_lemma = ifelse(str_detect(word_lemma, "\\d"), NA_character_, word_lemma), # Make sure digits are removed
           word_clean = ifelse(str_detect(word_clean, "\\d"), NA_character_, word_clean)) |>
    group_by(ID, word_raw) |>
    mutate(n_raw = n()) |>
    ungroup() |>
    group_by(ID, word_clean) |>
    mutate(n_clean = ifelse(is.na(word_clean), NA_integer_, n())) |>
    ungroup()
  
  type_df <- token_df |>
    group_by(ID, word_raw, word_lemma, word_clean) |>
    summarise(n_raw = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
    left_join(token_df |>
                filter(!is.na(word_clean)) |>
                group_by(ID, word_raw) |>
                summarise(n_clean = n(), .groups = "drop"), by = c("ID","word_raw"))
  
  type_df <- type_df |>
    arrange(ID, desc(n_clean), desc(n_raw))
  
  
  # Return base text (fully cleaned string-level text) (toggle on/off)
  if (return_base_text) {
    list(base_text = df_base$text, token_df = token_df, type_df = type_df)
  } else {
    list(token_df = token_df, type_df  = type_df)
  }
  
}

Prompt and animal key

Setting up the paired key for the FAST prompt terms, assigning them to affect or control terms and a positive, negative, or neutral valence. Also set up the animal key for the animal rankings, indicating which animals were depicted in the video content.

prompt_key <- tibble(prompt = c("teardrop", "pain", "happiness", "mirror", "knife", "family", "politician", "chicken", "eggs", "rain", "earthquake", "fall", 
             "crying", "suffering", "wellbeing", "reflection", "axe", "friends", "government", "cow", "milk", "sun", "hurricane", "spring"),
  
  prompt_class = c("affect_term", "affect_term", "affect_term", "control_term", 
                   "control_term", "control_term", "control_term", "stimulus_content", 
                   "stimulus_content", "control_term", "control_term", "control_term", 
                   "affect_term", "affect_term", "affect_term", "control_term", 
                   "control_term", "control_term", "control_term", "stimulus_content", 
                   "stimulus_content", "control_term", "control_term", "control_term"),
  
  valence = c("negative", "negative", "positive", "neutral", "negative", "positive", "neutral", "neutral", "neutral", "neutral", "negative", "positive", 
              "negative", "negative", "positive", "neutral", "negative", "positive", "neutral", "neutral", "neutral", "neutral", "negative", "positive"))

animals <- str_to_lower(c("cat", "chicken", "chimpanzee", "cow", "dog", "duck", "fish", "fly", "horse", 
                          "human", "lion", "monkey", "mouse", "pig", "rabbit", "sheep", "sparrow", "spider", "turkey"))

rating_types <- c("likeability", "similarity", "sentience")

depicted_animals <- c("chicken", "cow", "dog", "monkey", "pig", "sheep", "rabbit")

human_animal <- "human"

non_depicted_animals <- setdiff(animals, c(depicted_animals, human_animal))

animal_key <- tibble(animal = animals,
  depicted_status = case_when(animal %in% depicted_animals ~ "depicted_nonhuman", animal == human_animal ~ "human", TRUE ~ "non_depicted"))

Create FAST data frame

Creating FAST data frame, cleaning it, incorporating session order information, and checking for sufficient data.

fast_long <- fast_long_df |>
  group_by(SubID) |> mutate(response_num = row_number()) |> ungroup() |>
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("Fast"), names_to = "prompt_col", values_to = "response") |>
  mutate(session = case_when(str_detect(prompt_col, "^Fast1_") ~ 1L, str_detect(prompt_col, "^Fast2_") ~ 2L),
         prompt = str_remove(prompt_col, "^Fast[12]_"),
         response = str_to_lower(str_trim(response)),
         response = na_if(response, ""), response = na_if(response, "n/a"), response = na_if(response, "na")) |>
  left_join(subject_df |> select(SubID, Condition), by = "SubID") |>
  mutate(video_condition = case_when(Condition == 1 & session == 1 ~ "positive", 
                                     Condition == 1 & session == 2 ~ "negative", 
                                     Condition == -1 & session == 1 ~ "negative", 
                                     Condition == -1 & session == 2 ~ "positive"),
         session_order = case_when(Condition == 1 ~ "positive_first", 
                                   Condition == -1 ~ "negative_first"),
         session_order_coded = case_when(session_order == "negative_first" ~ -0.5, session_order == "positive_first" ~ 0.5)) |>
  left_join(prompt_key, by = "prompt") |>
  mutate(video_condition = factor(video_condition, levels = c("positive", "negative")),
         session_order = factor(session_order, levels = c("positive_first", "negative_first")),
         prompt_class = factor(prompt_class, levels = c("stimulus_content", "affect_term", "control_term")))

session_order_df <- fast_long |> distinct(SubID, session_order, session_order_coded)

fast_text_prompt <- fast_long |>
  filter(!is.na(response)) |>
  arrange(SubID, video_condition, prompt, response_num) |>
  group_by(SubID, session_order, session_order_coded, video_condition, session, prompt, prompt_class, valence) |>
  summarise(text = paste(response, collapse = " "), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(doc_id = row_number())

genie_prompt <- melanie_genie(data = fast_text_prompt, text_col = "text", id_col = "doc_id", lemmatize = TRUE, omit_stops = TRUE, remove_numbers = TRUE, remove_number_words = TRUE, remove_roman = TRUE, strip_possessives = TRUE, normalize_abbrev = TRUE, dash_mode = "prefix_merge", return_base_text = TRUE)
## Loading required package: koRpus.lang.en
## Loading required package: koRpus
## Loading required package: sylly
## 
## Attaching package: 'sylly'
## The following object is masked from 'package:psych':
## 
##     describe
## For information on available language packages for 'koRpus', run
## 
##   available.koRpus.lang()
## 
## and see ?install.koRpus.lang()
## 
## Attaching package: 'koRpus'
## The following object is masked from 'package:tm':
## 
##     readTagged
## The following objects are masked from 'package:quanteda':
## 
##     tokens, types
## The following object is masked from 'package:readr':
## 
##     tokenize
fast_tokens_clean <- genie_prompt$token_df |>
  filter(!is.na(word_clean)) |>
  left_join(fast_text_prompt |> select(doc_id, SubID, session_order, session_order_coded, video_condition, session, prompt, prompt_class, valence), by = c("ID" = "doc_id")) |>
  select(SubID, session_order, session_order_coded, video_condition, session, prompt, prompt_class, valence, id_row_orig, id_row_postsplit, word_raw, word_lemma, word_clean, n_raw, n_clean)

fast_tokens_clean |> count(video_condition, prompt_class) # woo balanced
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   video_condition prompt_class         n
##   <fct>           <fct>            <int>
## 1 positive        stimulus_content  5227
## 2 positive        affect_term       7569
## 3 positive        control_term     17989
## 4 negative        stimulus_content  5321
## 5 negative        affect_term       7635
## 6 negative        control_term     18143
# Check response balance across conditions/subjects
fast_tokens_clean |>
  count(SubID, video_condition, prompt_class) |>
  group_by(video_condition, prompt_class) |>
  summarise(mean_n = mean(n), sd_n = sd(n))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'video_condition'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
## # A tibble: 6 × 4
## # Groups:   video_condition [2]
##   video_condition prompt_class     mean_n  sd_n
##   <fct>           <fct>             <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 positive        stimulus_content   59.4  8.82
## 2 positive        affect_term        86.0 13.9 
## 3 positive        control_term      204.  23.8 
## 4 negative        stimulus_content   60.5  6.51
## 5 negative        affect_term        86.8 12.1 
## 6 negative        control_term      206.  21.4

Yay, the FAST data looks balanced. To the different paired prompts, there are ~18k words/responses for the positive and negative video condition control prompts (respectively), ~7k responses for affect prompts, and ~5k responses for stimulus prompts. The mean values are also similar within stimulus content, affect term, and control term across positive and negative video conditions.

Animal rankings dataframe, analyses

Building the animal ranking data frame for pre- and post-session rankings across the three categories (similarity, sentience, likeability) to see if there are significant changes.

# Helper function for animal dataframe based on the column names in subject_df
rank_name <- function(prefix, rt, animal){if(animal == "human") paste0(prefix, "_", rt, "_Human") else paste0(prefix, "_", rt, "_", animal)}

rank_df <- subject_df |> 
  select(SubID, Condition, starts_with("Rank1_"), starts_with("Rank2_")) |> 
  mutate(across(starts_with("Rank"), as.numeric))

for(rt in rating_types){for(animal in animals){pre_col <- rank_name("Rank1", rt, animal); post_col <- rank_name("Rank2", rt, animal); diff_col <- paste0("diff_", rt, "_", animal); if(pre_col %in% names(rank_df) & post_col %in% names(rank_df)){rank_df[[diff_col]] <- rank_df[[post_col]] - rank_df[[pre_col]]}}}

# Subject/trial-level long df for rankings... 
rank_long <- rank_df |>
  select(SubID, Condition, starts_with("diff_")) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("diff_"), names_to = c("rating_type", "animal"), names_pattern = "diff_(.+)_(.+)", values_to = "diff_score") |>
  left_join(animal_key, by = "animal") |>
  left_join(session_order_df, by = "SubID")

# Making summary df for plotting based on depicted/not-depicted status
rank_summary <- rank_long |>
  filter(depicted_status != "human") |>
  group_by(SubID, session_order, session_order_coded, rating_type, depicted_status) |>
  summarise(mean_diff = mean(diff_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(depicted_status = factor(depicted_status, levels = c("non_depicted", "depicted_nonhuman")))

# Mean df
animal_means <- rank_long |>
  group_by(animal, rating_type) |>
  summarise(mean_diff = mean(diff_score, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_diff = sd(diff_score, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")
animal_means |> arrange(mean_diff) |> head()
## # A tibble: 6 × 4
##   animal rating_type mean_diff sd_diff
##   <chr>  <chr>           <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 pig    similarity      -3.17    4.03
## 2 pig    likeability     -2.5     3.83
## 3 pig    sentience       -2.39    3.46
## 4 monkey similarity      -1.35    2.83
## 5 sheep  similarity      -1.31    3.61
## 6 sheep  sentience       -1.08    3.16
# Look at difference scores for all animals
rank_long |>
  group_by(animal, rating_type) |>
  summarise(mean_diff = mean(diff_score, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_diff = sd(diff_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(rating_type, desc(mean_diff))
## # A tibble: 57 × 4
##    animal  rating_type mean_diff sd_diff
##    <chr>   <chr>           <dbl>   <dbl>
##  1 fish    likeability    1.65      3.51
##  2 sparrow likeability    1.24      3.01
##  3 rabbit  likeability    0.875     3.77
##  4 duck    likeability    0.818     3.77
##  5 lion    likeability    0.511     2.72
##  6 horse   likeability    0.455     3.30
##  7 spider  likeability    0.375     2.14
##  8 sheep   likeability    0.318     4.03
##  9 human   likeability    0.273     2.16
## 10 mouse   likeability    0.0227    3.19
## # ℹ 47 more rows
# Plot depicted vs. not depicted, not considering human
rank_long |>
  filter(depicted_status != "human") |>
  ggplot(aes(diff_score, fill = depicted_status)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.8, position = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~rating_type) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "depicted_nonhuman" = "orange",
    "non_depicted" = "yellow2")) +
  theme_minimal()

Since 1 = “most liked/sentient/similar” and 19 = “least liked/sentient/similar”, negative difference scores mean that the ranking improved after watching the videos (ses2-ses1). The animal means df shows that pigs changed the most in ranking, becoming more likeable (mean of -2.5), sentient (-2.4), and similar (-3.2). The histogram of difference scores separated by depicted and non-depicted animals shows a normal distribution, though some depicted animals seem to have gotten better ratings after video watching as seen by the negative orange values.

Let’s do a t-test to see if it’s significant:

# Depicted vs. non-depicted animals, pre to post
rank_long |>
  filter(depicted_status != "human") |>
  group_by(depicted_status, rating_type) |>
  summarise(mean_diff = mean(diff_score, na.rm = TRUE))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'depicted_status'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
## # Groups:   depicted_status [2]
##   depicted_status   rating_type mean_diff
##   <chr>             <chr>           <dbl>
## 1 depicted_nonhuman likeability    -0.560
## 2 depicted_nonhuman sentience      -0.669
## 3 depicted_nonhuman similarity     -0.823
## 4 non_depicted      likeability     0.332
## 5 non_depicted      sentience       0.430
## 6 non_depicted      similarity      0.530
t.test(diff_score ~ depicted_status, data = rank_long |> filter(depicted_status != "human"))
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  diff_score by depicted_status
## t = -11.801, df = 3582.9, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group depicted_nonhuman and group non_depicted is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -1.299576 -0.929271
## sample estimates:
## mean in group depicted_nonhuman      mean in group non_depicted 
##                      -0.6839827                       0.4304408

Significant difference in rankings of depicted vs. non-depicted animals, t(3582.9) = -11.801, p < .001; depicted animals mean = -0.68 vs. non-depicted mean = 0.43.

Semantic distance with FAST data and analyses

Let’s run FAST data through SemanticDistance and see if

# Clean FAST text using SemanticDistance package
sd_cleaned <- clean_monologue_or_list(fast_text_prompt, "text", omit_stops = TRUE, lemmatize = TRUE)

# Word-level semantic distances
sd_out <- dist_ngram2word(dat = sd_cleaned, ngram = 1)

# Check output
names(sd_out)
##  [1] "SubID"               "session_order"       "session_order_coded"
##  [4] "video_condition"     "session"             "prompt"             
##  [7] "prompt_class"        "valence"             "doc_id"             
## [10] "id_row_orig"         "text_initialsplit"   "word_clean"         
## [13] "id_row_postsplit"    "CosDist_1gram_glo"   "CosDist_1gram_sd15"
sd_out |> count(video_condition, prompt_class)
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   video_condition prompt_class         n
##   <fct>           <fct>            <int>
## 1 positive        stimulus_content  5309
## 2 positive        affect_term       7735
## 3 positive        control_term     18440
## 4 negative        stimulus_content  5388
## 5 negative        affect_term       7784
## 6 negative        control_term     18511
# Average semantic distance for stimulus-related prompts only, removing NAs
sd_summary <- sd_out |>
  filter(prompt_class == "stimulus_content", !is.na(CosDist_1gram_glo)) |>
  group_by(SubID, session_order, session_order_coded, video_condition) |>
  summarise(mean_glo = mean(CosDist_1gram_glo, na.rm = TRUE),
            mean_sd15 = mean(CosDist_1gram_sd15, na.rm = TRUE),
            n_words = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Difference scores for condition context-effect: negative minus positive semantic distance for glove and sd15
semantic_context_df <- sd_summary |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = video_condition,
              values_from = c(mean_glo, mean_sd15, n_words)) |>
  mutate(semantic_context_glo = mean_glo_negative - mean_glo_positive,
         semantic_context_sd15 = mean_sd15_negative - mean_sd15_positive)

# Summary stats
semantic_context_df |> 
  summarise(mean_glo = mean(semantic_context_glo, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_glo = sd(semantic_context_glo, na.rm = TRUE),
            mean_sd15 = mean(semantic_context_sd15, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_sd15 = sd(semantic_context_sd15, na.rm = TRUE),
            n = n())
## # A tibble: 1 × 5
##   mean_glo sd_glo mean_sd15 sd_sd15     n
##      <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl> <int>
## 1 -0.00300 0.0789   -0.0304   0.118    88
head(semantic_context_df)
## # A tibble: 6 × 11
##   SubID  session_order  session_order_coded mean_glo_positive mean_glo_negative
##   <chr>  <fct>                        <dbl>             <dbl>             <dbl>
## 1 sub001 positive_first                 0.5             0.642             0.640
## 2 sub002 positive_first                 0.5             0.666             0.701
## 3 sub003 positive_first                 0.5             0.773             0.647
## 4 sub004 negative_first                -0.5             0.718             0.728
## 5 sub005 negative_first                -0.5             0.633             0.734
## 6 sub006 negative_first                -0.5             0.677             0.682
## # ℹ 6 more variables: mean_sd15_positive <dbl>, mean_sd15_negative <dbl>,
## #   n_words_positive <int>, n_words_negative <int>, semantic_context_glo <dbl>,
## #   semantic_context_sd15 <dbl>
# Test whether semantic context effects differ from zero
t.test(semantic_context_df$semantic_context_glo, mu = 0)
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  semantic_context_df$semantic_context_glo
## t = -0.35709, df = 87, p-value = 0.7219
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.01970964  0.01370616
## sample estimates:
##    mean of x 
## -0.003001739
t.test(semantic_context_df$semantic_context_sd15, mu = 0)
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  semantic_context_df$semantic_context_sd15
## t = -2.4161, df = 87, p-value = 0.01778
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.055386164 -0.005388976
## sample estimates:
##   mean of x 
## -0.03038757

Semantic context effect is defined as the difference in semantic distance between negative and positive video conditions (negative − positive). There was semantic context effect for the sd15 but not Glove. Semantic representations were tighter (i.e., lower semantic distance) following negative video content compared to positive content.

Big Kahuna: Are changes in animal rankings explaineed by changes in semantic representation?

Let’s see if changes in semantic representation explain the changes we see in animal rankings: drumroll please…..

analysis_df <- rank_summary |>
  left_join(semantic_context_df |> select(SubID, semantic_context_sd15), by = "SubID")

model <- lm(mean_diff ~ semantic_context_sd15 * depicted_status + session_order_coded, data = analysis_df)
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = mean_diff ~ semantic_context_sd15 * depicted_status + 
##     session_order_coded, data = analysis_df)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.1552 -0.4180 -0.0064  0.4579  2.8580 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                                        Estimate Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                             0.42779    0.05234
## semantic_context_sd15                                  -0.08723    0.43202
## depicted_statusdepicted_nonhuman                       -1.10809    0.07402
## session_order_coded                                    -0.03815    0.07169
## semantic_context_sd15:depicted_statusdepicted_nonhuman  0.20852    0.61082
##                                                        t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                                              8.173 2.28e-15 ***
## semantic_context_sd15                                   -0.202    0.840    
## depicted_statusdepicted_nonhuman                       -14.970  < 2e-16 ***
## session_order_coded                                     -0.532    0.595    
## semantic_context_sd15:depicted_statusdepicted_nonhuman   0.341    0.733    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.8233 on 523 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3166, Adjusted R-squared:  0.3114 
## F-statistic: 60.57 on 4 and 523 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Nope! Linear regression shows that while the animals depicted in the documentaries got better rankings, semantic context effects (using sd15) didn’t predict ranking changes, p = .84, and no interaction with depicted status, p = .60.

Exploratory

Plots from my final presentation; visual exploration of the data as word frequency by condition, if there was a session order effect, Null finding of Big Kahuna with sd15,

# Word frequency :) 
word_condition_diff <- fast_tokens_clean |>
  filter(prompt_class == "stimulus_content", !is.na(word_clean), word_clean != "") |>
  count(word_clean, video_condition) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = video_condition, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>
  mutate(total = negative + positive,
         diff = negative - positive) |>
  filter(total >= 15) |>
  arrange(desc(diff))

word_condition_diff |>
  slice_max(abs(diff), n = 30) |>
  mutate(direction = if_else(diff > 0, "More in negative", "More in positive"),
         word_clean = reorder(word_clean, diff)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = diff, y = word_clean, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "gray30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("More in negative" = "#D85A30", "More in positive" = "#1D9E75")) +
  labs(title = "Exploratory: Animal-related word frequency by video condition",
       subtitle = "Stimulus-content prompts only",
       x = "Frequency difference (negative − positive)",
       y = NULL,
       fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# Is there a session order effect?
semantic_context_df |>
  mutate(session_order = recode(session_order,
                                "negative_first" = "Negative first",
                                "positive_first" = "Positive first")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = session_order, y = semantic_context_sd15, fill = session_order)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = 21, width = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = session_order), width = 0.1, alpha = 0.45, size = 1.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Negative first" = "#D85A30", "Positive first" = "#1D9E75")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Negative first" = "#993C1D", "Positive first" = "#0F6E56")) +
  labs(title = "Exploratory: Semantic context effect by session order",
       subtitle = "Semantic context = negative − positive semantic distance",
       x = NULL,
       y = "Semantic context effect (sd15)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Big Kahuna lol 
analysis_df <- rank_summary |>
  left_join(semantic_context_df |> select(SubID, semantic_context_sd15), by = "SubID")

analysis_df |>
  mutate(rating_type = str_to_title(rating_type),
         session_order = recode(session_order,
                                "negative_first" = "Negative first",
                                "positive_first" = "Positive first")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = semantic_context_sd15, y = mean_diff)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = session_order), alpha = 0.55, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "gray20", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  facet_wrap(~rating_type, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Positive first" = "#1D9E75", "Negative first" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(title = "Exploratory: Semantic context and animal ranking change",
       subtitle = "Collapsed across depicted and non-depicted animals",
       x = "Semantic context effect (negative − positive; sd15)",
       y = "Mean ranking change (post − pre)",
       color = "Session order") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

##### Is FAST semantic distance different following positive vs negative condition?

h1_wide <- sd_out |>
  filter(prompt_class == "stimulus_content", !is.na(CosDist_1gram_sd15)) |>
  group_by(SubID, session_order, video_condition) |>
  summarise(mean_sd15 = mean(CosDist_1gram_sd15, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = video_condition, values_from = mean_sd15) |>
  mutate(diff = negative - positive)

t.test(h1_wide$diff, mu = 0)
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  h1_wide$diff
## t = -2.4138, df = 87, p-value = 0.01789
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.055376943 -0.005361672
## sample estimates:
##   mean of x 
## -0.03036931
model_sd15 <- lmer(mean_sd15 ~ video_condition + session_order + (1 | SubID), data = sd_summary)
summary(model_sd15)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: mean_sd15 ~ video_condition + session_order + (1 | SubID)
##    Data: sd_summary
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -212.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.54385 -0.45932  0.07129  0.49106  1.89881 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  SubID    (Intercept) 0.01492  0.12213 
##  Residual             0.00696  0.08343 
## Number of obs: 176, groups:  SubID, 88
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                             Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)                  0.59539    0.02139  27.831
## video_conditionnegative     -0.03039    0.01258  -2.416
## session_ordernegative_first -0.00352    0.02892  -0.122
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) vd_cnd
## vd_cndtnngt -0.294       
## sssn_rdrng_ -0.676  0.000
h1_wide |>
  group_by(session_order) |>
  summarise(
    mean_diff = mean(diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_diff = sd(diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    p = t.test(diff, mu = 0)$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   session_order  mean_diff sd_diff     n      p
##   <fct>              <dbl>   <dbl> <int>  <dbl>
## 1 positive_first   -0.0339   0.120    44 0.0667
## 2 negative_first   -0.0268   0.118    44 0.138
h1_plot_df <- h1_wide |>
  mutate(session_order = recode(session_order,
                                "negative_first" = "Negative first",
                                "positive_first" = "Positive first"))

ggplot(h1_plot_df, aes(x = session_order, y = diff, fill = session_order)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, width = 0.5, outlier.shape = 21) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Negative first" = "#D85A30", "Positive first" = "#1D9E75")) +
  labs(
    title = "FAST semantic context effect by condition",
    subtitle = "Difference = Negative − Positive sd15 semantic distance\nValues < 0 indicate lower semantic distance after negative videos",
    x = "Session order",
    y = "Semantic context effect (Negative − Positive)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

FAST responses following negative condition had lower semantic distance than those following positive condition, both with and without controlling for session order.
